text: Premature optimization is the root of all evil.
author: Donald Knuth
source: The Art of Computer Programming, Vol. 1

text: Simplicity is prerequisite for reliability.
author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

text: Computer science is no more about computers than astronomy is about telescopes.
author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

text: Bad programmers worry about the code. Good programmers worry about data and its relationships.
author: Linus Torvalds

text: Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place. Therefore, if you write the code as cleverly as possible, you are, by definition, not smart enough to debug it.
author: Brian Kernighan

text: Controlling complexity is the essence of computer programming.
author: Brian Kernighan

text: There are two ways of constructing a software design: one way is to make it so simple that there are obviously no deficiencies, and the other way is to make it so complicated that there are no obvious deficiencies.
author: C.A.R. Hoare

text: A program is like a poem: you cannot write a poem without writing it.
author: Alan J. Perlis

text: If you have a procedure with ten parameters, you probably missed some.
author: Alan J. Perlis

text: When I designed the shell, I knew I wanted it to be a programming language.
author: Ken Thompson

text: Unix is very simple, it just needs a genius to understand its simplicity.
author: Dennis Ritchie

text: Data dominates. If you've chosen the right data structures and organized things well, the algorithms will almost always be self-evident.
author: Rob Pike

text: Given enough eyeballs, all bugs are shallow.
author: Eric S. Raymond

text: Free software is a matter of liberty, not price. To understand the concept, you should think of free as in free speech, not as in free beer.
author: Richard Stallman

text: The programs that we use to build programs are themselves programs, and they are built by people. There is no magic.
author: Gerald Sussman

text: Lisp isn't a language, it's a building material.
author: John McCarthy

text: The von Neumann bottleneck has been the major limiting factor in computer performance over the last forty years.
author: John Backus
source: Can Programming Be Liberated from the von Neumann Style?

text: Adding manpower to a late software project makes it later.
author: Fred Brooks

text: Conceptual integrity is the most important consideration in system design.
author: Fred Brooks

text: The bearing of a child takes nine months, no matter how many women are assigned.
author: Fred Brooks

text: Let us change our traditional attitude to the construction of programs: instead of imagining that our main task is to instruct a computer what to do, let us concentrate rather on explaining to human beings what we want a computer to do.
author: Donald Knuth

text: From then on, when anything went wrong with a computer, we said it had bugs in it.
author: Grace Hopper

text: The most dangerous phrase in the language is, 'We've always done it this way.'
author: Grace Hopper

text: If you don't work on important problems, it's not likely that you'll do important work.
author: Richard Hamming

text: Luck favors the prepared mind.
author: Louis Pasteur

text: Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.
author: Harold Abelson
source: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs

text: Programs are meant to be read by humans and only incidentally for computers to execute.
author: Gerald Sussman

text: Sometimes, the elegant implementation is just a function. Not a method. Not a class. Not a framework. Just a function.
author: John Carmack

text: Story of my life: focused on the wrong problem.
author: John Carmack

text: It is much harder to make a complete, polished product than it is to make a cool demo.
author: John Carmack

text: Architecture is the decisions that you wish you could get right early in a project.
author: Ralph Johnson

text: All non-trivial abstractions, to some degree, are leaky.
author: Joel Spolsky

text: The First Law of Programming: any sufficiently advanced code is indistinguishable from magic. The Second Law: it isn't.
author: Jeff Atwood

text: Code is read more often than it is written.
author: Jeff Atwood

text: Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
author: Martin Fowler

text: When you feel the need to write a comment, first try to refactor the code so that the comment becomes superfluous.
author: Martin Fowler

text: Make it work, make it right, make it fast.
author: Kent Beck

text: I'm not a great programmer; I'm just a good programmer with great habits.
author: Kent Beck

text: Shipping first-time code is like going into debt. A little debt speeds development so long as it is paid back promptly.
author: Ward Cunningham

text: The Pragmatic Programmer: fix the problem, not the blame.
author: Andy Hunt

text: Don't leave broken windows.
author: Andy Hunt

text: It is better to have 100 functions operate on one data structure than 10 functions on 10 data structures.
author: Alan J. Perlis

text: Code generation, like drinking, is best done in moderation.
author: Tom Duff

text: Egoless programming is a philosophy where a programmer's ego is decoupled from the code they write.
author: Jerry Weinberg

text: If programmers are ego-involved with their programs, they are unlikely to improve.
author: Jerry Weinberg

text: It has long been an axiom of mine that the little things are infinitely the most important.
author: Charles Darwin

text: Those who don't understand Unix are condemned to reinvent it, poorly.
author: Henry Spencer

text: 640K ought to be enough for anybody.
author: Bill Gates

text: All problems in computer science can be solved by another level of indirection, except for the problem of too many layers of indirection.
author: David Wheeler

text: I made up the term object-oriented, and I can tell you I did not have C++ in mind.
author: Alan Kay

text: The big idea is 'messaging'. The key in making great and growable systems is much more to design how its modules communicate rather than what their internal properties and behaviors should be.
author: Alan Kay

text: Worse is better.
author: Richard P. Gabriel
source: Lisp: Good News, Bad News, How to Win Big

text: I think the way to become a better programmer is to read good code, write good code, and read about writing good code.
author: Steve Yegge

text: There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation and naming things.
author: Phil Karlton

text: 99 little bugs in the code, 99 little bugs in the code. Take one down, patch it around, 127 little bugs in the code.
author: Anonymous

text: The first 90 percent of the code accounts for the first 90 percent of the development time. The remaining 10 percent of the code accounts for the other 90 percent of the development time.
author: Tom Cargill
source: Bell Labs, quoted by Jon Bentley

text: The cliché of the 80s, 'knowledge is power', was never truer than in programming.
author: Kenneth Iverson
source: Notation as a Tool of Thought

text: Abstraction is a technique that helps us manage complexity, but it is not a substitute for understanding.
author: Barbara Liskov

text: The possibilities of the computer for augmenting human intellect are unlimited.
author: Douglas Engelbart

text: We can only see a short distance ahead, but we can see plenty there that needs to be done.
author: Alan Turing

text: Every aspect of learning or any other feature of intelligence can in principle be so precisely described that a machine can be made to simulate it.
author: John McCarthy
source: Proposal for the Dartmouth Summer Research Project on AI

text: Easy things are often hard.
author: Marvin Minsky

text: If you just learn a single trick, Scout, you'll get along a lot better with all kinds of folks. You never really understand a person until you consider things from his point of view.
author: Atticus Finch

text: Done is better than perfect, but done right is even better.
author: Jeff Sutherland

text: Legacy code is code without tests.
author: Michael Feathers

text: The only way to go fast, is to go well.
author: Robert C. Martin

text: Leave the code a little cleaner than you found it.
author: Robert C. Martin

text: Simple is not easy. Simple is not uncomplicated. Simple is the opposite of complex.
author: Rich Hickey

text: When you are complecting, you are braiding things together.
author: Rich Hickey

text: You should spend more time in the hammock. The hammock is where good design happens.
author: Rich Hickey

text: It's better to be approximately right than precisely wrong.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Stay hungry. Stay foolish.
author: Steve Jobs

text: You can't connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backwards.
author: Steve Jobs

text: C makes it easy to shoot yourself in the foot; C++ makes it harder, but when you do, it blows away your whole leg.
author: Bjarne Stroustrup

text: There are only two kinds of languages: the ones people complain about and the ones nobody uses.
author: Bjarne Stroustrup

text: There is more than one way to do it.
author: Larry Wall

text: I wanted to minimize my frustration with programming, because that's my primary goal. I want to have fun.
author: Yukihiro Matsumoto

text: Programmers often feel joy when they can concentrate on the creative side of programming, so Ruby is designed to make programmers happy.
author: Yukihiro Matsumoto

text: Code is a communication mechanism. It tells the computer what to do, but more importantly, it tells the next programmer what you were thinking.
author: Chris Lattner

text: Software engineering is the part of computer science that is too difficult for computer scientists.
author: Bertrand Meyer

text: If you do not take risks for your opinion, you are nothing.
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

text: The digital revolution is far more significant than the invention of writing or even of printing.
author: Douglas Engelbart

text: As far as the user is concerned, the interface is the product.
author: Jef Raskin

text: Most software today is very much like an Egyptian pyramid with millions of bricks piled on top of each other, with no structural integrity, but just done by brute force and thousands of slaves.
author: Alan Kay

text: The best way to predict the future is to invent it.
author: Alan Kay

text: A little inaccuracy can sometimes save a ton of explanation.
author: H. H. Munro

text: Programs = Algorithms + Data Structures.
author: Niklaus Wirth

text: The clear separation of algorithms and data structures is essential to good program design.
author: Niklaus Wirth

text: Before software can be reusable it first has to be usable.
author: Ralph Johnson

text: Don't program by coincidence.
author: Andy Hunt

text: The Boy Scout Rule: Leave the campground cleaner than you found it.
author: Robert C. Martin

text: Treat your servers like cattle, not pets.
author: Bill Baker

text: The computer is a medium for expressing our understanding of complex phenomena.
author: Gerald Sussman

text: Software is eating the world.
author: Marc Andreessen

text: The utility of a language as a tool of thought increases with the range of topics it can treat.
author: Kenneth Iverson
source: Notation as a Tool of Thought

text: In the long run, the best way to make progress is to keep your design simple.
author: Gregory Chaitin

text: Compilers are the bridge between human thought and machine execution.
author: Barbara Liskov

text: No matter what the problem is, it's always a people problem.
author: Jerry Weinberg

text: Organizations which design systems are constrained to produce designs which are copies of the communication structures of these organizations.
author: Melvin Conway

text: The programmer, like the poet, works only slightly removed from pure thought-stuff.
author: Fred Brooks

text: I would have to say that Quicksort was my first really good idea.
author: C.A.R. Hoare

text: Good algorithms are the closest thing we have to magic in computer science.
author: Robert Sedgewick

text: Be conservative in what you do, be liberal in what you accept from others.
author: Jon Postel

text: Errors using inadequate data are much less than those using no data at all.
author: Charles Babbage

text: The analytical engine weaves algebraic patterns just as the Jacquard loom weaves flowers and leaves.
author: Ada Lovelace

text: Imagination is the Discovering Faculty, pre-eminently. It is that which penetrates into the unseen worlds around us.
author: Ada Lovelace

text: We may hope that machines will eventually compete with men in all purely intellectual fields.
author: Alan Turing

text: If you can't explain why your code works, you don't understand it.
author: Gerald Sussman

text: There is no silver bullet.
author: Fred Brooks

text: He who refuses to write maintainable code is doomed to rewrite it.
author: Community lore

text: JSON is the serialization format of the internet.
author: Tim Bray

text: Software engineering must be based on principles of programming, not on principles of business management.
author: Niklaus Wirth

text: Any problem in computer science can be solved with another level of indirection.
author: David Wheeler

text: The computer is a medium, not a tool.
author: Alan Kay

text: The notation we use influences the way we think.
author: Kenneth Iverson

text: A ship in port is safe, but that's not what ships are built for.
author: Grace Hopper

text: We spent a great deal of time getting things to work.
author: J. Presper Eckert

text: If you read one book that's been read by millions, you'll be one of millions. If you read a book that almost nobody has read, you might just learn something new.
author: Alan Kay

text: The question of whether computers can think is like the question of whether submarines can swim.
author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

text: A well-written program is its own heaven; a poorly-written program is its own hell.
author: Geoffrey James

text: Write tests as if the next person to maintain the code is a violent psychopath who knows where you live.
author: Community lore

text: Programs are like wet cement: anything can be written, but some things can never be unwritten.
author: Community lore

text: If debugging is the process of removing bugs, then programming must be the process of putting them in.
author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

text: There are two ways of constructing a software design: One way is to make it so simple that there are obviously no deficiencies.
author: C.A.R. Hoare

text: We make tools, and the tools shape us. Our tools shape our children.
author: Alan Kay

text: The cost of adding a feature isn't just the time it takes to code it. The cost is the maintenance, documentation, and mental overhead for every future developer.
author: John Carmack

text: Test-Driven Development is a way of managing fear. Fear makes you hesitate. Fear makes you tentative. Fear makes you want to communicate less.
author: Kent Beck

text: A pragmatic programmer always questions their own assumptions.
author: Andy Hunt

text: When in doubt, print it out. When not in doubt, print it out anyway.
author: Community lore

text: Everyone knows that debugging is twice as hard as writing a program in the first place.
author: Brian Kernighan

text: Perfection is achieved not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: The enjoyment of one's tools is an essential ingredient of successful work.
author: Donald Knuth

text: Context is worth 80 IQ points.
author: Alan Kay

text: Elegance is not a dispensable luxury but a quality that decides between success and failure.
author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

text: Data is the new oil.
author: Clive Humby

text: Beauty is our business.
author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

text: Open source is a development methodology; free software is a social movement.
author: Eric S. Raymond

text: Code is law.
author: Lawrence Lessig
source: Code and Other Laws of Cyberspace

text: This is for everyone.
author: Tim Berners-Lee

text: The Web does not just connect machines, it connects people.
author: Tim Berners-Lee

text: The best way to predict the future is to invent it. The second-best way is to fund it.
author: Alan Kay

text: Specifications are a kind of code. They should be treated with the same care.
author: Rich Hickey

text: Software architecture is the set of design decisions that, if made incorrectly, may cause a project to fail.
author: Grady Booch

text: One accurate measurement is worth a thousand expert opinions.
author: Grace Hopper

text: The three laws of TDD: 1) You may not write production code until you have written a failing unit test. 2) You may not write more of a unit test than is sufficient to fail. 3) You may not write more production code than is sufficient to pass the failing test.
author: Robert C. Martin

text: If you haven't found the bug, you haven't found the bug.
author: David Wheeler

text: Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.
author: Gerald Sussman

text: The only way to learn a new programming language is by writing programs in it.
author: Brian Kernighan
